framework.name: Recursive Delegation Acknowledged as Unsolved
framework.description: OpenID explicitly lists recursive delegation (agent→sub-agent chains) as a future problem without solutions, not a current problem with failures. They have no scope attenuation mechanism. This confirms gap_3: nobody has done deep delegation chains because the auth infrastructure literally doesn't support it yet.
framework.raw_quote: Agents spawning sub-agents or communicating tasks to other agents create complex authorization chains without clear scope attenuation mechanisms.

source.title: [PDF] Identity Management for Agentic AI - OpenID
